# mtncloud_user (Resource)

Manages an MTN Cloud user in the caller's tenant. `password` and the Linux/Windows passwords are write-only (never returned by the API). Cross-tenant placement is not supported (it needs admin-accounts access the customer-admin token lacks).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A user assigned to one or more roles. The password is write-only.
resource "mtncloud_role" "operators" {
  name = "operators"
}

resource "mtncloud_user" "jdoe" {
  username = "jdoe"
  email    = "jdoe@example.com"
  password = var.user_password # set via a variable / secret, not in plain config

  first_name = "Jane"
  last_name  = "Doe"
  role_ids   = [mtncloud_role.operators.id]
}

variable "user_password" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `email` (String) Email address of the user.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password (write-only; never returned by the API).
- `role_ids` (List of Number) IDs of the roles assigned to the user.
- `username` (String) Username (login) of the user.

### Optional

- `first_name` (String) First name.
- `last_name` (String) Last name.
- `linux_keypair_id` (Number) Default Linux key pair ID.
- `linux_password` (String, Sensitive) Default Linux password (write-only).
- `linux_username` (String) Default Linux username for provisioned instances.
- `password_expired` (Boolean) Whether the password is marked expired (forces a reset at next login). Write-only directive.
- `receive_notifications` (Boolean) Whether the user receives notification emails.
- `windows_password` (String, Sensitive) Default Windows password (write-only).
- `windows_username` (String) Default Windows username for provisioned instances.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Numeric identifier of the user.
